name: Common Threads
summary: Common Threads is a First Nations-led Australian organisation that runs advocacy and campaign work on truth-telling, Treaty, land rights, and climate justice.
notes: Official organisation website clearly identifies mission and leadership.

name: SOSBlak Australia SA
summary: SOS Blak Australia is a grassroots Aboriginal rights campaign best known for mobilising against forced closure of remote Aboriginal communities.
notes: Domain is cited by SBS/NITV and Cultural Survival as the campaign site, but current activity and ownership are unclear.

name: No Underground Coal Gasification at Leigh Creek
summary: This campaign supports Adnyamathanha community opposition to underground coal gasification near Leigh Creek and Copley.
notes: Best match is a dedicated campaign page hosted by Original Power rather than a standalone domain.

name: Pormpuraaw Aboriginal Shire Council
summary: Pormpuraaw Aboriginal Shire Council is the local government authority delivering civic services and community development in Pormpuraaw.
notes: Official council website.

name: Lake Tyers Aboriginal Trust
summary: Lake Tyers Aboriginal Trust is an Aboriginal-governed body that manages trust land, governance, and community matters at Lake Tyers.
notes: Official trust website with governance and contact information.

name: University of Adelaide
summary: The University of Adelaide is a major South Australian university institution focused on higher education and research.
notes: The adelaide.edu.au domain is official and now reflects the post-2026 Adelaide University transition while retaining University of Adelaide context.

name: Mandingalby Yidinji Aboriginal Corporation
summary: Mandingalbay Yidinji Aboriginal Corporation is the native title representative body for Mandingalbay Yidinji people and their country near Cairns.
notes: Name appears with spelling variants (Mandingalby/Mandingalbay); this page is referenced via native title listings and provides direct MYAC details.

name: Nanum Wungthim Land & Sea Management Group
summary: Nanum Wungthim is an Indigenous ranger group in Napranum that delivers coastal, cultural heritage, and land-and-sea management work.
notes: No standalone organisation-run website found; used the National Indigenous Australians Agency profile page.

name: Lachlan Kalari River Aboriginal Water Council
summary: This entity appears to be an Aboriginal water governance and advocacy body associated with the Lachlan (Kalari) river region.
notes: No official organisation website located; this is a registry-style listing and explicitly states it is not the official website.

name: Government Architect NSW
summary: Government Architect NSW provides strategic design leadership to improve architecture, urban design, and landscape outcomes across NSW.
notes: Official NSW Government Architect website.

name: Lachlan Kalari River Aboriginal Water Committee
summary: This committee appears to be part of Aboriginal advisory structures informing water planning and management in the Lachlan region.
notes: Exact committee name is referenced in water-sector materials, but no dedicated committee-run site was found; this NSW Water page is the closest official program context.

name: Full Stop Australia
summary: Full Stop Australia is a national specialist service providing trauma-informed support and 24/7 counselling related to sexual, domestic, and family violence.
notes: Official organisation website.

name: Change the Record
summary: Change the Record is a First Nations-led coalition campaigning to end over-incarceration and systemic violence against Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.
notes: Official coalition website.

name: Wenlock Catchment Management Group Inc
summary: Wenlock Catchment Management Group Inc is a Cape York catchment group focused on river and landscape stewardship in the Wenlock region.
notes: No standalone website found; group is listed through Queensland Water & Land Carers and referenced in partner conservation content.

name: Mounty Aboriginal Youth and Community Service
summary: Mounty Aboriginal Youth and Community Service is an Aboriginal-controlled Mt Druitt youth service supporting community-led programs and justice reinvestment outcomes.
notes: No clear standalone domain was indexed; this appears to be the primary public page and may require Facebook login.

name: Western Sydney Watch Committee
summary: The Watch Committee monitors Aboriginal treatment in custody and advocates on deaths in custody, policing, and justice-system accountability.
notes: Search results tie this to the Aboriginal Deaths in Custody Watch Committee page hosted by Justice Action; specific Western Sydney branding is limited.

name: Our Islands Our Home
summary: Our Islands Our Home is a Torres Strait Islander-led climate justice campaign seeking action to protect Zenadth Kes from climate impacts.
notes: Official campaign website.

name: Mehi Centre
summary: Mehi Centre is a not-for-profit based in Moree that supports Indigenous communities through cultural, educational, environmental, and advocacy initiatives.
notes: Official website includes organisation description and ABN.

name: Martuwarra Fitzroy River Council
summary: Martuwarra Fitzroy River Council is an Indigenous-led alliance of Kimberley Elders advocating to protect the Martuwarra/Fitzroy River system.
notes: Official council website.

name: National Tertiary Education Union
summary: The National Tertiary Education Union is the main union representing university and tertiary education workers across Australia.
notes: Official union website.

name: Fisher's Oysters Pty Ltd
summary: Fishers of Freycinet is a Tasmania-based seafood and oyster business associated with the Fisher family’s farmed oyster operations.
notes: An exact indexed website for the legal entity name 'Fisher's Oysters Pty Ltd' was not clearly found; this is the closest active brand/domain match.
